description: Buyer-readiness checklist for Chinese warehouse automation vendors preparing a first Italian pilot. It defines proof to prepare for buyer review and does not certify legal compliance.

items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: Operations and engineering teams need operating limits before allowing equipment into a live warehouse pilot.
items.minimum_content_expected: Payload, dimensions, speed, battery runtime, charging requirements, operating environment, floor requirements, connectivity, and known operating limits.

items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: The buyer will likely ask for a clear safety readiness summary before reviewing equipment that operates near people, forklifts, goods, or production flows.
items.minimum_content_expected: CE status or readiness statement, safety features, emergency-stop behavior, operating limits, human interaction assumptions, and documents available for buyer review.

items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: Warehouse managers need to understand how setup avoids disrupting dispatch, production, operators, or client service commitments.
items.minimum_content_expected: Site preparation steps, timeline, mapping or commissioning plan, off-peak work assumptions, rollback path, buyer responsibilities, and launch checklist.

items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: The buyer needs assurance that basic faults, wear parts, and support needs will not stop the pilot for an international lead time.
items.minimum_content_expected: Preventive maintenance tasks, remote diagnostics approach, critical spare list, Italy or EU spare-part availability, lead times, and ownership during the pilot.

items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: Operations needs defined response expectations if the automation affects live warehouse work.
items.minimum_content_expected: Support hours, response time target, escalation contacts, remote support channel, on-site support option, language coverage, and first-week support plan.

items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: Operations and IT need to know how tasks, exceptions, and data move between the warehouse system and the automation workflow.
items.minimum_content_expected: Minimum viable integration mode, task trigger, data exchanged, API or CSV option, operator tablet fallback, exception handling, and go-live test steps.

items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: Finance and operations need a business case tied to the buyer's own labor, walking time, throughput, error, or manual-handling baseline.
items.minimum_content_expected: Baseline metric, pilot KPI target, shift and labor assumptions, operational saving logic, sensitivity notes, and buyer data needed to replace assumptions.

items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: The buyer needs success criteria that operations and finance can review together before expanding beyond the pilot.
items.minimum_content_expected: Baseline measurement window, target KPIs, data owner, measurement cadence, success thresholds, and weekly review process.

items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: The buyer needs a practical summary of pilot-area risks before allowing automated equipment into mixed warehouse traffic.
items.minimum_content_expected: Pilot route map, pedestrian and forklift interaction points, emergency-stop locations, restricted areas, residual risk owner, and pre-launch safety checklist.

items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: A clear responsibility and exit path lowers procurement risk and makes a first pilot easier to approve.
items.minimum_content_expected: Pilot duration, success criteria, failure criteria, rollback or removal terms, responsibility for faults, warranty boundaries, and conversion decision point.

items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: The buyer needs proof that the product has worked in a similar warehouse process, even if an Italian reference customer does not exist yet.
items.minimum_content_expected: Customer type, warehouse process, deployment scope, measured outcome, before-and-after metric, and why the case is transferable to the Italian pilot.

items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: Operators and supervisors need simple guidance for onboarding, daily use, exceptions, and escalation during the pilot.
items.minimum_content_expected: Quick-start guide, supervisor checklist, safety do and do not list, exception handling, daily startup and shutdown, and first-week training plan.

items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: IT teams need to understand what operational data is collected, where it is processed, and who can access it during the pilot.
items.minimum_content_expected: Data collected, storage location, remote access approach, user roles, retention period, and deletion or export process.

items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: Pharma and healthcare logistics buyers may need to understand how the vendor supports quality review and change-control discussion.
items.minimum_content_expected: Validation scope, change-control support, audit-trail behavior, deviation handling, document owner, and pilot limitation statement.
